title: juniperus virginiana subsp. silicicola
intro: Juniperus virginiana subsp. silicicola, also known as Eastern Red Cedar, is a subspecies of coniferous tree native to the eastern United States. The essential oil extracted from its wood and foliage has been used for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and diuretic properties. This plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the urinary system. The herbal preparations of Juniperus virginiana subsp. silicicola include essential oils, extracts, and teas, which can be used topically or ingested under medical guidance.
